snow_or_rain_notes: Snow patches on the ridge for the first few weeks of May if the previous winter had heavy snowfall
First spell of snowfall expected for the last groups of November due to western disturbances
Post-monsoon season (September) follows monsoon outpour, leaving trail lush green
temperature_notes: Summer daytime: 12°C to 17°C; night drops to 0°C; 3 warm layers required
Autumn daytime: 10°C to 14°C; night drops to 0°C to -3°C; 4 warm layers required

terrain_challenges: Steep ascent through forest sections on day 1 gaining ~3000 ft over 4-5 km
Exposed and windy ridge requiring layering for wind chill
Steep climb from Bhoj Gadi to the Phulara Ridge taking close to 1 hour
Tricky climb onto the ridge in early May or June if there is snow
Tricky descent from the ridge on the Pushtara side if there is too much snow
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek reaches 12,345 ft; above 10,000 ft AMS risk is real
Day 1 elevation gain of ~3000 ft over 4-5 km coupled with exertion increases susceptibility to Acute Mountain Sickness
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; even fit people climbing too high too soon are at equal risk
Itinerary on other days allows gradual elevation gain and acclimatisation
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can significantly alter trek dynamics

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Cardiovascular Endurance and Muscle Strength (core, calves, glutes, quadriceps, hamstrings)
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month

experience_themes: Vibrant meadows of Pushtara in summer
Carpet of flowers in full bloom in June
Lush green trail in September fresh from monsoon
primary_watchouts: Phulara Ridge crossing is dependent on snow and weather conditions on the day of crossing
In bad weather or unsafe ridge conditions, trek leader may decide to return from Bhoj Gadi instead of crossing
Steep ascent through forest sections on day 1 gaining ~3000 ft over 4-5 km
Exposed and windy ridge requiring layering for wind chill
Steep climb from Bhoj Gadi to the Phulara Ridge taking close to 1 hour
Tricky climb onto the ridge in early May or June if there is snow
best_for: Viewing blooming rhododendrons and flower-filled meadows
